similarity_name: Structural descriptor or latent embedding similarity
definition: Represent each crystal by a structural descriptor, such as orbital field matrix features, local atomic-environment features, or a learned graph-model latent vector, then compute cosine similarity between evaluation and training structures.
example_usage: python embedding_cosine_similarity.py --train train_structures.csv --eval eval_structures.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --embedding-cols-prefix struct_

citations.title: Structure-Based Out-of-Distribution Materials Property Prediction: A Benchmark Study

evidence.summary: The paper argues random splits overestimate materials-property performance because of redundancy and high train-test similarity.
OOD target sets are constructed from sparse regions of structure or property space using structural features and clustering.
Latent spaces combine structure and composition information and cluster materials with similar properties.

limitations: Learned embeddings can encode model biases and should not be treated as ground-truth structural distance.
Descriptor choice changes the scientific meaning of distance.
Feature scaling and missing structural data can dominate similarity.
quality_gates: Record descriptor type, feature normalization, and whether features are learned or hand-engineered.
Check that near-duplicate structures are not leaking across train and evaluation sets.
